---
title: "FedRAMP RFCs — UIAO substrate readiness assessment (2026-05)"
finding_id: "FINDING-003"
status: Open
severity: P2
created_at: "2026-05-10"
updated_at: "2026-05-10"
owner: "governance-steward"
related_canon: ["UIAO_022", "UIAO_023", "UIAO_110", "UIAO_111", "UIAO_113", "UIAO_131", "UIAO_132", "UIAO_133", "UIAO_140", "UIAO_200"]
related_adrs: ["ADR-033", "ADR-043", "ADR-047", "ADR-054"]
related_findings: ["FINDING-001", "FINDING-002"]
supersedes: []
superseded_by: []
---

# FedRAMP RFCs — UIAO substrate readiness assessment

Synthesis of the UIAO substrate's posture against the open and
recently-closed FedRAMP Requests for Comment published at
[fedramp.gov/rfcs](https://www.fedramp.gov/rfcs/), with per-RFC
gaps and a prioritized remediation backlog. This is a governance
assessment, not a finding about a single external constraint —
it is filed under `docs/findings/` because the artifact class fits
(structured, evidence-backed, owner-attributed) and several rows
below escalate into proper FINDING-NNN entries on adoption.

The assessment is anchored on UIAO at the commit at the head of
`claude/assess-fedramp-uiao-8a6Cr` (post-PR #349). The RFC corpus
covered is RFC-0005 through RFC-0030 inclusive, with the public
20x pilot framework (FedRAMP 20x Phase 2 Moderate path) treated
as the umbrella program the RFCs feed.

## 1. RFC corpus and UIAO posture summary

| RFC | Topic | Comment period | UIAO posture | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| RFC-0005 | Minimum Assessment Scope Standard | Closed 2025-05-25 | **Specified, not enforced.** UIAO_133 §3 defines the `mas-scope` frontmatter field and a classification rubric; classification is not yet applied across all canon documents and there is no CI gate that rejects unclassified canon edits. | P2 |
| RFC-0006 | 20x Phase 1 KSIs | Closed 2025 | **Reference-only.** Phase 1 KSIs are catalogued in `canon/data/fedramp-20x.yml`; no substrate emission targets the Phase 1 catalog. | P3 |
| RFC-0008 | Continuous Reporting Standard | Closed 2025-06-09 | **Partially implemented.** ConMon CLI (`conmon-process`, `conmon-export-oa`, `conmon-dashboard`) emits ongoing-authorization records; mitigated-vulnerability rerating is not yet wired through `evidence/poam.py`. | P2 |
| RFC-0010 | Continuous Reporting Best Practices | Pending | **Awaiting publication.** ADR-047 ratification is gated on this RFC. | P3 |
| RFC-0012 | Continuous Vulnerability Management Standard | Closed | **Pathway-2 only.** ADR-043 wires the traditional pathway (monthly OS / DB / web / container / service-config scans, monthly POA&M, annual IA scan). VDR Balance Improvement Release pathway is pre-wired but gated. | P2 |
| RFC-0014 | 20x Phase 2 KSIs | Closed | **Specified, not emitted.** UIAO_133 §2.2 maps 11 substrate outputs to KSI themes; the OSCAL generator at `src/uiao/oscal/generator.py` does not yet inject the four `fedramp:*` `props`. | **P1** |
| RFC-0019–0023 | Authorization-process updates (designations, marketplace, external frameworks, no-sponsor program) | Closed Q1 2026 | **Acknowledged, not actioned.** No ADR records the FedRAMP-Ready retirement deadline (2026-07-28); the substrate's authorization-state model still assumes the legacy Ready/Authorized two-state vocabulary. | P2 |
| RFC-0024 | Rev5 Machine-Readable Packages | Closed 2026-03-11 | **Partial.** UIAO emits OSCAL POA&M, SSP, and Assessment Results (`generators/{poam,ssp,sar}.py`, `generators/trestle.py` for compliance-trestle assembly) and CycloneDX SBOM (`generators/sbom.py`). No documented Trust Center / API surface for agency consumption, no validation against the FedRAMP-published machine-readable schema, no integration with the FedRAMP submission pipeline. Effective date 2026-09-30. | **P1** |
| RFC-0026 | CA-7 Continuous Monitoring Expectations | Closed 2026-04-22 | **Accepted and integrated.** ADR-043 (ACCEPTED 2026-04-27), UIAO_132 spec drafted, Pathway 2 wired, Pathway 1 pre-wired. Enforcement clock: 2027-01-01. | OK |
| RFC-0027 | Rev5 baseline update — AC, AT, AU, CA, CM | Open (added 2026-03-19) | **Not ingested.** Existing control-library coverage in `canon/data/control-library/{ac,at,au,ca,cm}/` totals 79 controls; the proposed Additional FedRAMP Security Technical Controls Requirements deltas have not been merged into the YAML. | P2 |
| RFC-0028 | Rev5 baseline update — CP, IA, IR, MA, MP | Open (added 2026-03-19) | **Not ingested.** 66 controls in scope across these families. The MA "where applicable" nationality-documentation issue called out in the RFC comments directly impacts UIAO's contractor-access narratives. | P2 |
| RFC-0029 | Rev5 baseline update — PE, PL, PM, PS, PT | Open (added 2026-03-19) | **Blocked — control families missing.** `pe/`, `pl/`, `ps/` exist (33 controls); **`pm/` and `pt/` directories are absent from `canon/data/control-library/`**. UIAO cannot produce a complete Moderate SSP from canon at this date. | **P1** |
| RFC-0030 | Rev5 baseline update — RA, SA, SC, SI, SR | Open (added 2026-03-19) | **Blocked — SR family missing.** `ra/`, `sa/`, `sc/`, `si/` exist (69 controls); **`sr/` (Supply Chain Risk Management) is absent from `canon/data/control-library/`** despite SBOM emission via `generators/sbom.py` and supply-chain references in SA-4, RA-9, RA-3, IR-6(3). | **P1** |

> Posture grades:
> - **OK** — substrate satisfies the RFC requirement at its published effective date.
> - **P3** — known gap, no near-term enforcement clock.
> - **P2** — known gap with an enforcement clock more than 90 days out.
> - **P1** — known gap blocking a near-term enforcement clock or an emit-side dependency the program-level FINDING-002 already calls out.

## 2. UIAO capabilities verified during this assessment

The substrate already ships the following machinery the RFCs presuppose:

| Capability | Source | RFCs satisfied (in part) |
|---|---|---|
| OSCAL POA&M + SSP excerpt generator | `src/uiao/oscal/generator.py` | RFC-0024 |
| OSCAL SSP / SAR / SBOM generators | `src/uiao/generators/{ssp,sar,sbom}.py`; `generators/trestle.py` for compliance-trestle assembly | RFC-0024 |
| KSI evaluator + 10-rule library | `src/uiao/ksi/evaluate.py`, `src/uiao/ksi/rules/KSI-001..010.yaml` | RFC-0006 (partial), RFC-0014 (partial) |
| ConMon ongoing-authorization generator | `src/uiao/monitoring/ongoing_auth.py` | RFC-0008, RFC-0026 |
| Sentinel hook / event processor | `src/uiao/monitoring/{event_processor,sentinel_hook}.py` | RFC-0008, RFC-0026 |
| Five-class drift taxonomy + walker | `src/uiao/substrate/walker.py`, `docs/docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd` | RFC-0014 (DRIFT-EVIDENCE-STALE planned) |
| Adapter registry with class × mission-class taxonomy | `src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml`, `modernization-registry.yaml` | RFC-0024 (component-definition source) |
| GCC-Moderate boundary as canonical drift class | ADR-033, `gcc-boundary-gap-registry.yaml` | RFC-0005 (boundary input) |
| Single-ATO reciprocity model | ADR-054, UIAO_140 | RFC-0019/0021/0022 (reuse posture) |
| Rev5 CA-7 Pathway 2 wiring | ADR-043, UIAO_132 | RFC-0026 |
| Auditor bundle pipeline (evidence → SSP narrative + lineage + POA&M) | `src/uiao/auditor/bundle.py` | RFC-0008, RFC-0024 |

## 3. Gaps blocking near-term RFC compliance

### 3.1 Missing control families (RFC-0029, RFC-0030) — **P1**

`canon/data/control-library/` is missing three families: **`pm/`** (Program
Management), **`pt/`** (Personally Identifiable Information Processing
and Transparency), and **`sr/`** (Supply Chain Risk Management).

- PM and PT are scoped by RFC-0029.
- SR is scoped by RFC-0030 and is required by FedRAMP Moderate even
  though several SR-adjacent controls (SA-4, RA-9, IR-6(3)) carry
  supply-chain language in their narratives.
- The substrate cannot produce a structurally complete OSCAL SSP for
  Rev5 Moderate without these. `generators/ssp.py` builds from the
  `canon/data/control-library/` tree; missing families surface as
  empty `implemented-requirement` arrays on submission.

**Remedy:** add the three family directories with at minimum the
Moderate-baseline controls (PM-1..PM-32 selected, PT-1..PT-8 selected,
SR-1..SR-12 selected), and extend `tests/test_control_library.py` to
fail when any FedRAMP Moderate baseline control is absent.

### 3.2 OSCAL emissions missing FedRAMP `props` (RFC-0014, RFC-0024) — **P1**

UIAO_133 §2.1 specifies four required props (`fedramp:ksi-themes`,
`fedramp:ksi-mapping-source`, `fedramp:ksi-freshness-cadence`,
`fedramp:ksi-emitted-at`) on every emitted OSCAL artifact. The
generator at `src/uiao/oscal/generator.py` does not yet inject them,
and the validation harness UIAO_133 §2.4 specifies has no
implementation under `tests/`.

**Remedy:** wire the four props at the emission boundary in
`oscal/generator.py` (deterministic — derive from the §2.2 emission
map keyed by `Responsible component`); add the schema-check and
mapping-source-check tests under `tests/test_oscal_emission_props.py`.

### 3.3 KSI catalog impedance mismatch (RFC-0006, RFC-0014) — **P1**

UIAO ships a 10-rule KSI library (`KSI-001`..`KSI-010`) that is
SCuBA-anchored and uses a private namespace (e.g. KSI-001 "Multi-Factor
Authentication Enforcement", source field `MFAEnabled`). The FedRAMP
20x KSI catalog uses theme-prefixed identifiers
(`KSI-AUTH`, `KSI-CMT`, `KSI-CNA`, `KSI-CED`, `KSI-IAM`, `KSI-INR`,
`KSI-MLA`, `KSI-PIY`, `KSI-RPL`, `KSI-SVC`, `KSI-SCN`).

There is no machine-readable crosswalk between the two namespaces in
canon. `docs/data/ksi-mappings.yml` carries hand-written ksi entries
keyed by an unrelated `KSI-IDENTITY-MFA` namespace, deepening the
collision.

**Remedy:** add `src/uiao/canon/data/ksi-crosswalk.yaml` mapping every
local `KSI-001..010` rule to one or more FedRAMP 20x KSI themes, plus
the `M365-…` SCuBA policy IDs the rules already cite. Validate the
crosswalk in `tests/test_ksi_crosswalk.py` and reference it from the
`fedramp:ksi-mapping-source` prop wired in §3.2.

### 3.4 RFC-0024 Trust Center / API submission surface — P2

RFC-0024 effective date for accepting machine-readable submissions is
**2026-09-30**. UIAO emits OSCAL artifacts but has no documented
endpoint that exposes them via the FedRAMP-compatible Trust Center
API contract referenced in Notice 0009. The FastAPI service in
`src/uiao/api/` (gated behind the `[api]` extra) is the natural mount
point, and the auditor bundle in `src/uiao/auditor/bundle.py` already
produces all of `evidence-bundle.json`, `poam.json`, `ssp-narrative.md`,
and `lineage.json` — but no API route surfaces them.

**Remedy:** add `/oscal/{ssp,poam,sar,sbom}` GET endpoints to
`src/uiao/api/` (signed, agency-tenant-scoped via the existing
single-ATO reciprocity model in ADR-054). Document the contract in a
new spec under `src/uiao/canon/specs/fedramp-trust-center-api.md`,
allocate UIAO_141, and gate enforcement on RFC-0024 final guidance.

### 3.5 RFC-0023 — FedRAMP Ready retirement (2026-07-28) — P2

RFC-0023's outcome (Notice 0008) retires FedRAMP Ready. UIAO's
authorization-state model still assumes Ready/In-Process/Authorized;
references in `canon/data/fedramp-20x.yml` and several ADRs predate
the retirement.

**Remedy:** add an ADR ("FedRAMP Ready retirement and authorization-state
model update") that (a) records the deadline, (b) updates the
authorization-state vocabulary to In-Process / Program-Certified /
Agency-Authorized / Decommissioned, and (c) audits the ~12 canon
documents that name "Ready".

### 3.6 RFC-0027/0028/0029/0030 baseline-text deltas — P2

The four open RFCs propose textual updates to the Additional FedRAMP
Security Technical Controls Requirements and Guidance. None of the
proposed deltas are merged into `canon/data/control-library/`. The
families with existing directories will need diff review against the
RFC text (79 + 66 + 33 + 69 = 247 controls); the missing families
(PM, PT, SR) will need de-novo authoring per §3.1.

**Remedy:** open four tracking ADRs (one per RFC), each with a
narrow scope: ingest the RFC's proposed text into the canon control
narratives once the comment period closes and FedRAMP publishes
final guidance. Block-on: the comment-period close dates (currently
unspecified beyond "added 2026-03-19").

### 3.7 RFC-0008 mitigated-vulnerability rerating — P2

RFC-0008 explicitly encourages reporting mitigated vulnerabilities
at their estimated post-mitigation risk rating rather than their
original rating. UIAO's POA&M generator at `src/uiao/evidence/poam.py`
records `severity` from the source finding and does not carry a
`risk-status-mitigated` field.

**Remedy:** extend the POA&M record schema with optional
`severity_post_mitigation` + `mitigation_evidence_id` fields;
default both to null; surface them via the `conmon-export-oa`
output. Backfill is not required — the field starts populating
forward from the schema bump.

### 3.8 NTC-0003 — Automated Security Inbox — P3 (deadline already past)

`canon/data/fedramp-20x.yml` records NTC-0003 (Automated Security
Inbox, deadline 2026-01-05) as "Required" but the substrate has no
inbox endpoint. The deadline has passed; this is a documentation
debt that needs either an exception ADR or a referral to the
agency-side mailbox of record for the GCC-Moderate boundary.

## 4. Recommended action ordering

1. **Land the three missing control families (PM, PT, SR)** as YAML
   stubs aligned to the FedRAMP Moderate baseline. This is a
   structural prerequisite for everything downstream and unblocks
   RFC-0029/0030 ingestion.
2. **Wire the four FedRAMP `props` in the OSCAL generator** and add
   the validation harness under `tests/`. This satisfies the most
   concrete RFC-0014/RFC-0024 contract UIAO_133 already specifies.
3. **Land the KSI crosswalk** (`canon/data/ksi-crosswalk.yaml`) so
   that step 2's `fedramp:ksi-mapping-source` prop has a stable
   target.
4. **Open the four RFC-0027/0028/0029/0030 tracking ADRs** with
   narrow scope and external-trigger gates.
5. **Specify the Trust Center API surface** in a new spec
   (UIAO_141) and prototype the four GET endpoints under
   `src/uiao/api/`.
6. **POA&M post-mitigation severity** field bump (small, isolated).
7. **FedRAMP Ready retirement ADR** (documentation-only, but
   visible).
8. **NTC-0003 exception ADR** (documentation-only).

Items 1–3 are the only **P1**-graded items; items 4–8 are **P2/P3**
documentation and integration work.

## 5. Out of scope for this assessment

- 20x Phase 2 Moderate Pilot agency-sponsor selection — agency-side
  decision tracked in [FINDING-002](./fedramp-20x-moderate-pilot.qmd).
- Microsoft GCC-Moderate telemetry feature parity — vendor-side
  constraint tracked in [FINDING-001](./fedramp-gcc-moderate-informed-network-routing.qmd)
  and the broader GCC-Moderate finding family.
- 3PAO selection and engagement — out-of-band per ADR-025 §D5.

## 6. Status tracking

| Date | Event | Note |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-05-10 | Created | Synthesis of fedramp.gov/rfcs (RFC-0005..RFC-0030) against UIAO at HEAD of `claude/assess-fedramp-uiao-8a6Cr`. |
